concept.conceptName: Urea | Serum or Plasma | Chemistry - non-challenge
Urea | Serum, Plasma or Blood | Chemistry - non-challenge
Urea/Creatinine [Molar ratio] in Serum or Plasma
Urea/Creatinine [Mass Ratio] in Serum or Plasma

en.name: Urea in blood
en.longDescription: ## Definition & Clinical Context Urea is the main nitrogenous end-product of protein catabolism; its concentration in blood is used to assess renal function and the protein catabolic state [1]. In US sources it is often reported as **blood urea nitrogen (BUN)** — the nitrogen content of urea, which is **not numerically equal** to urea itself (see Mapping Notes below). The source data will typically be a single numeric result per patient-timestamp, produced by a biochemistry analyser. Results are reported as a substance concentration in mmol/L (SI unit) or as a mass concentration in mg/dL [2]. This concept set aggregates urea measurements across blood specimens (serum, plasma, arterial, venous and unspecified blood). ## Included Concepts The set is anchored on five LOINC Hierarchy concepts (one per blood specimen, scoped to `Chemistry - non-challenge`), with their descendants pulled in. After exclusions, the set resolves to eight LOINC "Lab Test" concepts, grouped here by specimen. ### Serum or plasma (default specimen for routine biochemistry) - *3020779 / 22664-7 — Urea [Moles/volume] in Serum or Plasma (LOINC)*: most widely used LOINC code worldwide. - *3034204 / 3091-6 — Urea [Mass/volume] in Serum or Plasma (LOINC)*: same measurand expressed as mass concentration. - *3042602 / 39776-0 — Urea [Moles/volume] in Serum or Plasma --post dialysis (LOINC)*: point-in-time measurement immediately after a haemodialysis session. - *3039815 / 39777-8 — Urea [Moles/volume] in Serum or Plasma --pre dialysis (LOINC)*: point-in-time measurement immediately before a haemodialysis session. ### Generic blood (specimen unspecified) - *43534077 / 72903-8 — Urea [Moles/volume] in Blood (LOINC)* - *3009810 / 20977-5 — Urea [Mass/volume] in Blood (LOINC)*: same measurand expressed as mass concentration. - *46235781 / 77136-0 — Urea [Moles/volume] in Serum, Plasma or Blood (LOINC)*: specimen-agnostic. ### Arterial blood - *43534101 / 72927-7 — Urea [Moles/volume] in Arterial blood (LOINC)* ### Venous blood - *43534100 / 72926-9 — Urea [Moles/volume] in Venous blood (LOINC)* **Note on units.** Recommended unit follows LOINC's `EXAMPLE_UCUM_UNITS`: **`mmol/L`** (concept 8753 — millimole per litre) for `[Moles/volume]` codes and **`mg/dL`** (concept 8840) for `[Mass/volume]` codes. Conversion: `1 mmol/L urea ≈ 6.01 mg/dL urea` (molar mass of urea = 60.06 g/mol). ## Excluded Concepts The Serum/Plasma hierarchy above pulls in two LOINC concepts that are explicitly excluded: - *3040431 / 39783-6 — Urea/Creatinine [Molar ratio] in Serum or Plasma*: derived ratio, not a urea concentration. - *40760116 / 56997-0 — Urea/Creatinine [Mass Ratio] in Serum or Plasma*: derived ratio, not a urea concentration. The set also deliberately omits the following LOINC concepts: - **Urine specimens**: out of scope (captured by the separate "Urinary urea" concept set). - **Body fluid, CSF and other specimens**: out of scope for a "blood" concept set. ## Mapping Notes - **Default concept** when the source reports "urea", "urée" without further specification: `3020779 / 22664-7 — Urea [Moles/volume] in Serum or Plasma`. By far the most common LOINC code. - **Source reports BUN (blood urea nitrogen)**: BUN measures only the nitrogen content of urea (2 N atoms × 14 g/mol = 28 g/mol). Stoichiometric conversion: `1 mg/dL BUN ≈ 0.357 mmol/L urea` and `1 mmol/L urea ≈ 2.8 mg/dL BUN` [3]. **Do not map a BUN value directly to a urea LOINC code without converting**, otherwise the value will be off by a factor of ~2.14. - **Do not confuse with**: urea nitrogen / BUN (uses different LOINC codes, conversion factor required), urinary urea, urea/creatinine ratio. ## References [1] Regenstrief Institute. LOINC code 22664-7 — Urea [Moles/volume] in Serum or Plasma. Available: https://loinc.org/22664-7/ [2] Nomenclature for Properties and Units (NPU). IFCC/IUPAC. NPU codes (current release). Available: https://npu-terminology.org/npu-database/ [3] Hosten AO. BUN and Creatinine. In: Walker HK, Hall WD, Hurst JW, editors. *Clinical Methods: The History, Physical, and Laboratory Examinations*. 3rd ed. Boston: Butterworths; 1990. Chapter 193. Available: https://www.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/books/NBK305/
